news.t: From Coke to GM: Decode the Q1 Earnings Wave Through ETFs
Two ETFs that have beaten value stock indexes with this simple approach
If you think stocks have gotten too expensive, this ETF's strategy may work for your portfolio
These Unloved Stocks Could Bounce 20%+ By 2022
ETFs with big Cisco stakes fall as network-equipment giant's stock set for worst day in 9 years

holdings.infoBox: PKW is an equity ETF with a total of 227 individual holdings. The top holdings are Citigroup stock at 6.05%, Wells Fargo & Company at 5.03%, Johnson Controls International at 4.57%, Adobe at 3.85%, and General Motors Company at 3.49%.

overview.description: The Invesco Buyback Achievers ETF (PKW) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the NASDAQ US Buyback Achievers index, a market-cap-weighted index of stock in US firms that repurchased at least 5% of their outstanding shares in the previous 12 months. PKW was launched on Dec 20, 2006 and is issued by Invesco.

data.text: While the markets are generally fixated on what the Magnificent Seven is doing in terms of first-quarter earnings, there are other names investors may want to track. This morning's earnings bonanza wa...

data.title: ETFs with big Cisco stakes fall as network-equipment giant's stock set for worst day in 9 years
data.text: Shares of Cisco Systems Inc. CSCO, -11.20% slid more than 11% Thursday, on track for their worst day since February 2011, taking a few exchange-traded funds along with them.

data.title: 3 ways to cash in on earnings season
data.text: Tom Lydon of ETF Trends.com and Ric Edelman of Edelman Financial Engines talk the transports kicking off earnings season with CNBC's Bob Pisani.
